libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Keep JVM tests and native-image builds configured with --add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED when exercising Guice injection/proxy/AOP paths. The 2.9.1 main artifact jarjar-renames internal cglib/util classes with '$' simple names (for example $MethodProxy, $Signature, $MapMaker), so tests should prefer public Guice APIs and must not import the old unprefixed internal classes from 2.1.7.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8858 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.sonatype.sisu:sisu-guice:2.9.1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: The org.slf4j:slf4j-api dependency is optional and upstream core tests explicitly run without it, so adding it is not required for normal coverage.

The sisu-guice:nodeps classifier contains original non-JarJar classes for extension compilation, but it is not the normal runtime artifact and adding it would risk testing a different classpath or duplicate Guice classes.
Local Gradle and Native Image verification remain authoritative for whether the add-opens setting is sufficient on the target JDK.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra Maven dependency or Docker service is needed, but meaningful Guice proxy/AOP coverage on modern JDKs requires opening java.lang because sisu-guice 2.9.1 uses embedded cglib reflection.
